callback_timing.c(3) Library Functions Manual callback_timing.c(3)

NAME

callback_timing.c - WebSocket callback timing instrumentation implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void websocket_callback_timing_record (websocket_callback_stats_t *stats, uint64_t start_ns, uint64_t end_ns)
Record callback invocation. void websocket_callback_timing_log_stats (void)
Log current callback statistics. void websocket_callback_timing_reset (void)
Reset timing statistics.

Variables


websocket_callback_timing_t g_ws_callback_timing = {0}
Global timing tracker - shared across all WebSocket connections.

Detailed Description

WebSocket callback timing instrumentation implementation.

Implements callback timing tracking for diagnosing WebSocket performance issues.

Definition in file callback_timing.c.

Function Documentation

void websocket_callback_timing_log_stats (void )

Log current callback statistics. Prints aggregate statistics for all tracked callbacks to help diagnose callback frequency and performance issues.

Definition at line 66 of file callback_timing.c.

66                                                {
67   uint64_t protocol_init_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_init.count);
68   uint64_t protocol_destroy_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_destroy.count);
69   uint64_t writeable_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.server_writeable.count);
70   uint64_t receive_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.receive.count);
71 
72   log_info("\n===== WEBSOCKET CALLBACK TIMING STATISTICS =====");
73   log_info("Timestamp: %lu ns", websocket_callback_timing_start());
74 
75   // PROTOCOL_INIT stats
76   log_info("LWS_CALLBACK_PROTOCOL_INIT:");
77   log_info("  Total invocations: %lu", protocol_init_count);
78   if (protocol_init_count > 0) {
79     uint64_t total_duration = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_init.total_duration_ns);
80     uint64_t avg_duration = total_duration / protocol_init_count;
81     log_info("  Avg duration: %lu ns", avg_duration);
82   }
83 
84   // PROTOCOL_DESTROY stats
85   log_info("LWS_CALLBACK_PROTOCOL_DESTROY:");
86   log_info("  Total invocations: %lu", protocol_destroy_count);
87   if (protocol_destroy_count > 0) {
88     uint64_t total_duration = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_destroy.total_duration_ns);
89     uint64_t avg_duration = total_duration / protocol_destroy_count;
90     log_info("  Avg duration: %lu ns", avg_duration);
91   }
92 
93   // SERVER_WRITEABLE stats
94   log_info("LWS_CALLBACK_SERVER_WRITEABLE:");
95   log_info("  Total invocations: %lu", writeable_count);
96   if (writeable_count > 0) {
97     uint64_t total_duration = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.server_writeable.total_duration_ns);
98     uint64_t avg_duration = total_duration / writeable_count;
99     uint64_t min_interval = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.server_writeable.min_interval_ns);
100     uint64_t max_interval = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.server_writeable.max_interval_ns);
101     log_info("  Avg duration: %lu ns", avg_duration);
102     if (min_interval != UINT64_MAX) {
103       log_info("  Min interval between callbacks: %lu ns (%.2f Hz)", min_interval, 1e9 / (double)min_interval);
104     }
105     if (max_interval > 0) {
106       log_info("  Max interval between callbacks: %lu ns (%.2f Hz)", max_interval, 1e9 / (double)max_interval);
107     }
108   }
109 
110   // RECEIVE stats
111   log_info("LWS_CALLBACK_RECEIVE:");
112   log_info("  Total invocations: %lu", receive_count);
113   if (receive_count > 0) {
114     uint64_t total_duration = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.receive.total_duration_ns);
115     uint64_t avg_duration = total_duration / receive_count;
116     uint64_t min_interval = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.receive.min_interval_ns);
117     uint64_t max_interval = atomic_load_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.receive.max_interval_ns);
118     log_info("  Avg duration: %lu ns", avg_duration);
119     if (min_interval != UINT64_MAX) {
120       log_info("  Min interval between callbacks: %lu ns (%.2f Hz)", min_interval, 1e9 / (double)min_interval);
121     }
122     if (max_interval > 0) {
123       log_info("  Max interval between callbacks: %lu ns (%.2f Hz)", max_interval, 1e9 / (double)max_interval);
124     }
125   }
126 
127   log_info("===== END TIMING STATISTICS =====\n");
128 }




References atomic_load_u64(), g_ws_callback_timing,
    and log_info.






void
  websocket_callback_timing_record (websocket_callback_stats_t * stats,
  uint64_t start_ns, uint64_t end_ns)


Record callback invocation. Updates statistics for the given
    callback type with timing information.




Parameters


stats Pointer to callback
  statistics structure


start_ns Start timestamp from websocket_callback_timing_start()


end_ns End timestamp (typically current time)


Definition at line 21 of file callback_timing.c.


21                                                                                                              {
22   if (!stats) {
23     return;
24   }
25 
26   uint64_t duration_ns = (end_ns > start_ns) ? (end_ns - start_ns) : 0;
27   uint64_t count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&stats->count, 1);
28 
29   // Track cumulative duration
30   atomic_fetch_add_u64(&stats->total_duration_ns, duration_ns);
31 
32   // Update last timestamp (atomically swap)
33   uint64_t prev_last_ns = atomic_load_u64(&stats->last_ns);
34   while (!atomic_cas_u64(&stats->last_ns, &prev_last_ns, end_ns)) {
35     // Retry if CAS failed due to concurrent update
36   }
37 
38   // Calculate interval from previous callback (if not the first one)
39   if (count > 0 && prev_last_ns > 0) {
40     uint64_t interval_ns = (end_ns > prev_last_ns) ? (end_ns - prev_last_ns) : 0;
41 
42     // Track min intervals (atomic compare-and-swap loop to avoid TOCTOU race)
43     if (interval_ns > 0) {
44       uint64_t current_min = atomic_load_u64(&stats->min_interval_ns);
45       while (interval_ns < current_min) {
46         if (atomic_cas_u64(&stats->min_interval_ns, &current_min, interval_ns)) {
47           break;
48         }
49         // CAS failed - reload current value and retry if still applicable
50         current_min = atomic_load_u64(&stats->min_interval_ns);
51       }
52     }
53 
54     // Track max intervals (atomic compare-and-swap loop to avoid TOCTOU race)
55     uint64_t current_max = atomic_load_u64(&stats->max_interval_ns);
56     while (interval_ns > current_max) {
57       if (atomic_cas_u64(&stats->max_interval_ns, &current_max, interval_ns)) {
58         break;
59       }
60       // CAS failed - reload current value and retry if still applicable
61       current_max = atomic_load_u64(&stats->max_interval_ns);
62     }
63   }
64 }






References atomic_cas_u64(), atomic_fetch_add_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), and stats.






void
  websocket_callback_timing_reset (void )


Reset timing statistics. Clears all counters and timing
    information. Useful for baseline measurements.


Definition at line 130 of file
  callback_timing.c.


130                                            {
131   memset(&g_ws_callback_timing, 0, sizeof(g_ws_callback_timing));
132   atomic_store_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_init.min_interval_ns, UINT64_MAX);
133   atomic_store_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.protocol_destroy.min_interval_ns, UINT64_MAX);
134   atomic_store_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.server_writeable.min_interval_ns, UINT64_MAX);
135   atomic_store_u64(&g_ws_callback_timing.receive.min_interval_ns, UINT64_MAX);
136 }






References atomic_store_u64(), and
    g_ws_callback_timing.
